_meta.purpose: Maps brief acronyms to their Google-indexed alternative meanings. SKILL.md Step 3b reads this file during brief intake — when an acronym from this dict appears in the brief's Industry / Product / Audience fields with the brief's intended meaning DIFFERENT from one or more entries here, the skill auto-suggests adding the collisions to the Exclusions list as phrase negatives.
_meta.purpose_short: Catch invisible budget burn from same-acronym different-meaning Google query indexing.
_meta.operator_edit_instructions: Add new entries as you encounter acronym collisions in dogfood. Each entry: {acronym (uppercase), intended_meaning (when used in a brief in this domain), collisions (list of phrase negatives to surface), confidence (high/medium = always suggest; low = only when budget < $200/day to avoid noise)}.
_meta.case_sensitivity: Match the acronym case-INSENSITIVELY in the brief text — operators write 'PIP' or 'pip' or 'Pip' interchangeably.

PIP.intended_meaning: Personal Injury Protection (US auto insurance, no-fault coverage)
PIP.evidence: 2026-05-16 Primary Urgent Care dogfood — operator added all 6 as phrase negatives post-launch.

EMC.intended_meaning: Emergency Medical Condition (Florida no-fault qualifier for full PIP)
EMC.evidence: 2026-05-16 Primary Urgent Care dogfood — operator added all 3 as phrase negatives post-launch.

IRS.intended_meaning: Industry-Recognized Standard / Information Retrieval System (any technical vertical)
IRS.evidence: 2026-05-16 Primary Urgent Care dogfood — operator flagged 'irs' / 'tax' as recurring junk-traffic queries triggered by ambiguous account naming.

PT.evidence: Common medical-vertical ambiguity; surface when brief Industry mentions medical/healthcare AND budget < $300/day.

ER.evidence: Lower-frequency collision; only surface when budget is very tight (< $150/day) AND brief Audience contains 'patient' / 'medical'.

OB.evidence: Rare collision; only surface in tightly-budgeted medical campaigns.

MVA.evidence: Generally safe acronym in medical context — collision is niche marketing jargon.

RE.intended_meaning: Real Estate (when brief Industry = real estate)
RE.evidence: Only surface when budget < $100/day; otherwise normal phrase context disambiguates.

AC.evidence: HVAC vertical specific; AC repair queries get polluted by electrical-engineering content.

ED.intended_meaning: Emergency Department (medical) OR Erectile Dysfunction (DTC health) — DEPENDS on Audience context
ED.evidence: Always surface when brief contains 'ED' — operator must confirm which meaning + add the other set as negatives.
